name: Sauteed Pork Chops with Sauerkraut
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy saucepan, cook the bacon over moderate heat, stirring until it is crisp.
2. Add the onion and caraway seeds to the saucepan and cook the mixture, stirring until the onion is golden.
3. Add sauerkraut and apple juice to the saucepan and simmer the mixture, covered, for 20 minutes.
4. While the sauerkraut mixture is simmering, sprinkle both sides of the pork chops with salt. Let the chops stand between layers of paper towel for 10 minutes.
5. Season the chops with pepper.
6. In a heavy skillet, heat oil over moderately high heat until it is hot but not smoking.
7. Sauté the pork chops for 5-6 minutes on each side, or until they are just cooked through.
8. Stir 1 tablespoon of dill into the sauerkraut mixture and transfer the mixture with the chops to serve.
9. Sprinkle the chops with the remaining 1 tablespoon dill before serving.
